purpose: Evaluate tools for bulk-harvesting Ancestry.com DNA match cousin trees into GEDCOM or CSV
target_use_case: Import ~25 closest DNA match trees from Ancestry

critical_context.ancestry_api_lockdown: June/July 2020 — Ancestry sent cease-and-desist letters to ALL third-party tools accessing DNA match data
critical_context.outcome: Genetic Affairs Ancestry access permanently dead; DNAGedcom operates under obfuscation ('A* Company'); Pedigree Thief Ancestry dead; browser extensions dead

tools.name: DNAGedcom Client

cost.price_2026: Unlisted publicly — requires account login to view current pricing; historically $5/month Silver

tools.mfa_handling: Not explicitly documented; Ancestry 2FA may block client access
tools.ancestry_reference: Uses term 'A* Company' to obscure Ancestry branding

output.match_list: m_profilename.csv — match name, cM, segments, tree size, tree URL, notes, ethnicity, admin info
output.ancestor_file: a_profilename.csv — direct ancestors extracted from each match's linked public tree (combined flat CSV, all matches merged)
output.note: Outputs are flat CSVs for bulk analysis. GWorks web tool cross-references surnames and finds common ancestors. No individual per-cousin GEDCOM export.

tools.ancestry_tos_posture: EXPLICITLY BANNED — received C&D from Ancestry in June 2020. Continued operating via obfuscation.
tools.risk_account_suspension: Moderate. No widespread user account bans reported. Tool rate-limits itself. Low-volume harvest (25 matches) is lower risk.

speed.note: GWorks upload and processing adds additional time (15-30 minutes)

tools.hunter_fit: Best available automated option. Gets ancestor pedigrees from match trees as a flat CSV for GWorks analysis. Not per-cousin GEDCOMs.
user_feedback_2024_2026: Jan 2025 Reddit r/Genealogy: 'I see that they have re-vamped the user interface but indeed many of the download features are now unavailable. I'm currently grabbing all my data off of A(ncestry).'
Jul 2025 dna-explained.com: Listed as 'DNAGedcom – Clusters results from various vendors' (active tool reference)
Jan 2025 Reddit: 'Maybe a decade ago... always some kind of problems, and basically zero instructions. Even though I had a subscription I wasn't using it at all.'
signup_steps: 2. Click 'Create an account' or go to /Account/Register.aspx
6. Log out and back in to enable Silver features
7. Download Windows or Mac client from homepage
8. Run client, set output folder, enter DNAGedcom login
9. Select Ancestry ('A* Company') on main page
11. Select profile, click 'Gather Matches' then 'Gather Trees'
12. Upload resulting CSV files to dnagedcom.com for GWorks analysis

tools.name: Genome Mate Pro (Genealogical DNA Analysis Tool)
tools.note: Required DNAGedcom Client output as input. No standalone Ancestry tree access. Rarely mentioned in 2024-2026 discussions.

tools.name: Borland Genetics
tools.type: Web platform for ancestral DNA reconstruction

cost.model: Free basic; subscription for 'Creeper' automated reconstruction feature

tools.ancestry_access: DNA upload from Ancestry raw data only — no match tree downloading
tools.output: Reconstructed ancestral DNA kits, not tree files
tools.hunter_fit: Wrong tool entirely — DNA reconstruction database, not tree harvesting

tools.name: MyHeritage Family Tree Builder

cost.model: Free app; MyHeritage subscription for full matching features

tools.note: Standard genealogy software; imports GEDCOM; syncs with MyHeritage tree only; no Ancestry match tree access. MyHeritage stopped accepting DNA uploads from other vendors May 7, 2025.
tools.hunter_fit: Wrong tool — no Ancestry integration for match trees

tools.name: GEDmatch / GEDmatch Genesis
tools.owner: Verogen Inc (subsidiary of Qiagen, acquired Jan 2023)
tools.type: Web platform for cross-vendor DNA comparison
tools.note: Users upload raw DNA files from any vendor. GEDmatch's database is users who have uploaded to GEDmatch — completely separate from Ancestry's database. Genetic Affairs licensed tools (AutoCluster, AutoKinship) available within GEDmatch for Tier 1 users.
tools.hunter_fit: Wrong tool for Ancestry tree harvesting; useful if matches also have GEDmatch kits

tools.name: Genetic Affairs
tools.status: Active, actively developed 2026. Licensed by MyHeritage and GEDmatch.

cost.subscription: Required for AutoLineage desktop app; pricing not publicly listed

tools.ancestry_access: Indirect only — manual HTML file save per match required. Direct automated Ancestry access dead since July 2020 C&D.
tools.ancestry_support_for_ftdna: Full automation available for FamilyTreeDNA matches (Feb 2026)
tools.output: Cluster visualizations, AutoKinship relationship trees, GEDCOM-importable outputs via AutoLineage
tools.hunter_fit: Partial — requires manual HTML saves for each Ancestry match (25 manual saves = feasible but tedious). Better if matches also have FTDNA kits.

tools.name: Pedigree Thief (Chrome Extension)
tools.status: Listed in Chrome Store but Ancestry support confirmed dead
tools.note: Kitty Cooper confirmed: 'Pedigree Thief works on most sites, just not Ancestry.' Works on MyHeritage, WikiTree. Outputs ahnentafel text, convertible to GEDCOM via kittymunson.com/dna/Ahnen2GEDcom.php

tools.name: FamilySearch Family Tree
tools.note: No Ancestry DNA match system integration. Valuable for record research and building your own tree. 2025 full-text search is major feature.
tools.hunter_fit: Wrong tool for tree harvesting from Ancestry matches

tools.name: Ancestry ProTools (native 2025 feature)

cost.model: Requires ProTools subscription AND Ancestry family history membership or AncestryDNA Plus

tools.output: Match clustering visualization (grid view), shared DNA display, ThruLines. Does NOT export trees, does NOT download match trees.
tools.hunter_fit: Useful complement to manual tree work but does not harvest trees

rank_1.limitations: Outputs flat CSV ancestor list across all matches, not per-cousin GEDCOMs. GWorks analysis is done on DNAGedcom website.
rank_1.tos_risk: Moderate (explicit C&D 2020, no ban reports for individual users)

rank_2.approach: Manual — visit each match's linked tree, use Save to Tree to copy key ancestors
rank_2.output: Directly populates YOUR Ancestry research tree; can then export as GEDCOM

rank_3.approach: Semi-manual — save each match's tree page as HTML, import to AutoLineage
rank_3.tos_risk: Low (manual saves are not automated scraping)

recommended_action.primary: DNAGedcom Client v4.0.445 for bulk ancestor pedigree CSV harvest + GWorks analysis
recommended_action.secondary: Manual 'Save to Tree' in Ancestry for the 5-10 matches with the best/most relevant trees
recommended_action.download_url: Linked from dnagedcom.com homepage after login
recommended_action.time_total: 2-4 hours for full harvest of 25 closest matches including upload and GWorks analysis
recommended_action.tos_warning: DNAGedcom received an Ancestry C&D in 2020 and continues to operate via obfuscation ('A* Company'). Individual user account bans have not been widely reported. Run on a weekend, rate-limit your session, do not run simultaneously with other Ancestry activity.

sources.title: DNAGedcom Homepage (2026)
sources.key_finding: v4.0.445 current; A* Company (Ancestry) listed as supported; MyHeritage and GEDmatch listed as not working

sources.title: Kitty Cooper Blog: Solving unknown parentage cases with DNA
sources.key_finding: Step-by-step GWorks guide; Silver subscription required; Gather Trees creates a_profilename.csv ancestor file

sources.title: DataMiningDNA: A Comparison of Tools to Download Ancestry Matches
sources.key_finding: Ancestry blocked all third-party tools June 2020; DNAGedcom Silver $5/month; ancestor file included in download

sources.title: DNAeXplained: Ancestry to Remove DNA Matches Soon
sources.key_finding: Confirms Ancestry C&D letters sent June 2020 to ALL third-party tools including Genetic Affairs and DNAGedcom; DNAGedcom 'still functioning'

sources.title: DNAeXplained: How to Use Ancestry's New Match Clusters
sources.key_finding: Ancestry ProTools clustering launched 2025; DNAGedcom listed as active for clustering; no tree download feature in ProTools

sources.title: DNAeXplained: AutoKinship by Genetic Affairs (FTDNA)
sources.key_finding: Genetic Affairs full automation for FTDNA; Ancestry requires manual HTML file import only; 200 free credits on signup; subscription for AutoLineage

sources.title: DNAeXplained: 2025 Genetic Genealogy Retrospective
sources.key_finding: Year in review: DNAGedcom not mentioned as major player; Genetic Affairs cited as 'tried and true staple'; no new tree harvesting tools noted

sources.title: Reddit r/Genealogy: Chromosome Mapping (Jan 2025)
sources.key_finding: User reports DNAGedcom UI re-vamped, still downloading Ancestry data; another user found it clunky with poor instructions

sources.title: Genetic Affairs Homepage (2026)
sources.key_finding: Active; licensed by MyHeritage and GEDmatch; 200 free credits on signup; credit-based pricing

sources.title: Wikipedia: GEDmatch
sources.key_finding: Tier 1 premium $10/month; owned by Verogen Inc (Qiagen subsidiary since Jan 2023); 1.45M DNA profiles as of Fall 2020

sources.title: Kitty Cooper Blog: Collecting Family Trees with Automation
sources.key_finding: Pedigree Thief works on most sites BUT NOT Ancestry; manual Save to Tree still viable for small numbers
